statement.en: The Fine-Structure Constant and the Golden Angle
statement.summary: The fine-structure constant α ≈ 1/137.036 has no derivation in the Standard Model. Thispaper presents a four-step derivation from the Canon unified field theory’s pressure axiom tothe golden angle θφ = 360ř/φ2 ≈ 137.508ř, closes the 0.47ř gap through torsional slip in afunneled-spring recursion (Version 3), and now adds the slip-time interpretation (Version 4).The slip-time interpretation gives a physical reading of the single open parameter g/ω.Torsion does not slip instantaneously — it accumulates over a finite coherence interval beforethe spring releases. The golden angle is the infinite-time attractor: what the field would reachif coherence were unlimited and torsion never slipped. The fine-structure constant is thefinite-time attractor: the packing angle produced when torsion accumula

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper presents a provisional theoretical framework (Version 4, dated 2026) attempting to derive a fundamental physical constant from first principles via non-standard unified field theory. The work is explicitly iterative ('Version 3→4'), contains speculative mechanisms (torsional slip, funneled-spring recursion) not yet validated by peer review or experimental evidence, and closes a gap (0.47°) through ad-hoc parametrization rather than derivation. No published validation or reproducible experimental confirmation available in record.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 黄金角と微細構造定数は、ねじれが時間内に滑り落ちる速度で分離する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 金色のらせんが無限時間の引き込みと有限時間の脱落の間で張力を保つ。
